category_title: Low-attaining students who exhibit off-task behaviors like coloring or tearing paper during fast-paced student-led discussions
scenario_title: Explaining a fraction strategy using physical rods
minimal_context: David is using physical rods to explain his strategy for a fraction task during a whole-class discussion at the end of the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: David, you had a strategy for working out the fraction task using the physical rods to make a whole. Please explain it to the class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What did you discover about the rod that fit in the gap?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: It was a third… no… half.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: David, you had a strategy for working out the fraction task using the physical rods to make a whole. Please explain it to the class.

STUDENT: I did…um

TUTOR: Can you remember?

STUDENT: I… I…

TUTOR: What did you discover about the rod that fit in the gap?

STUDENT: It was a third… no… half.

task.content: Explaining a strategy for a fraction task using physical rods (Cuisenaire-style) to fill a gap to make a 'whole'

context.environment: Whole-class discussion at the end of a lesson

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When invited to explain his strategy and asked 'Can you remember?'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Struggled to articulate strategy, hesitated, and changed answer
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Seemed to find articulating his strategy difficult
related_quotes: It was a third… no… half.

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to articulate clearly what they had done or thought
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Unable to articulate clearly what they had done or thought
